one_line_thesis: DataSection is betting the Neo Cloud franchise on hyperscaler-linked AI data centres in Japan (Inzai) and Australia, with a FY2026 revenue step-up to ¥33.6B still guided post-Apr-2026 delays; the May 2026 shift to NVIDIA B300 via Compal (~USD 325M) and a disclosed financing bridge improves execution visibility but raises contract-renegotiation, debt-timing, and Giga-order overhang risk.

deep_dive.executive_summary: DataSection is attempting a step-change from small legacy revenue to hyperscaler-scale AI data-center operations in FY2026. Management has named capacity, capex, and a financing bridge in English IR filings (inventory tier; PDF extract still failed), but durable competitive advantage is unproven and asymmetric payoff (Pabrai dhando) fails on concentrated capex and customer concentration. At ¥2,591 (Yahoo, 2026-07-21), base expected annual return is 13.98% on normalized ¥90/share owner earnings (FY2026 guided EPS discounted for ramp risk), with proof-first component base ¥2,953/sh. Upside re...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

timeline.label: Secured 5,080 B300 GPUs via Compal (~USD 325M); disclosed financing bridge (advance, borrowings, 23rd rights)
Revised FY2026 forecast: revenue ¥33,601M, OP ¥3,635M, parent EPS ¥115.57 (ramp year)
Revenue guide cut 9.9% vs January 2026 forecast on delayed GPU delivery; profit revised up on timing
Cancelled 22nd stock-acquisition rights (¥818M share-based comp benefit)
Disclosed GPU scale ambition (100k–300k GPUs YE2026 aim, non-binding marketing)
AI data-center revenue begins (interim H1 FY2025: AI DC revenue from Sep 2025)
FY2025 consolidated revenue ¥2,942M; pivot from legacy IT toward Neo Cloud platform
Neo Cloud / TAIZA AI data-center strategy articulated in English IR materials

industry.structure: Japanese neo-cloud AI infrastructure is concentrated among a handful of operators competing for hyperscaler and enterprise GPU tenancy; capital intensity and supply-chain access (NVIDIA/ODM partners) are primary entry barriers.
industry.share_shift: Hyperscalers and global ODMs gain share through in-house capacity and scale; regional neo-cloud players like DataSection compete on location, timing, and customer relationships rather than proven cost advantage.
industry.trend: AI training and inference demand is driving a GPU datacenter buildout in Japan; B300/B200 upgrade cycles and financing bridges create execution volatility for smaller operators.

top_claims.claim: DataSection is attempting a step-change from small legacy revenue to hyperscaler-scale AI data-center operations in FY2026. Management has named capacity, capex, and a financing bridge in English IR filings (inventory tier; PDF extract still failed), but durable competitive advantage is unproven and asymmetric payoff (
We expect **13.98%** per year over 7 years at **¥2,591** on **¥90/sh** starting owner cash.
*Primary risk: Financing gap if customer advance lags Compal delivery (May to July 2026) while Giga USD 252M overhang absorbs management bandwidth.
Contract repricing after B300 upgrade with hyperscaler counterparty.
